response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine strengths in high-throughput distributed systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic) and is actively building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at his current role, which directly aligns with the AI-native engineering environment. However, Python — listed as a must-have — is entirely absent from his resume, which is a material gap at Staff level. His staff titles are real but the evidence of driving cross-functional technical strategy at scale is thin; the work reads more as senior IC with broad breadth than strategic technical leadership. He is a credible maybe pending a conversation to probe the Python gap and the depth of his strategy influence.
strengths: Direct, recent experience building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at re:cinq, matching the AI-native engineering baseline the role requires
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems work at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O design
Strong software craftsmanship discipline — TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, design patterns — applied consistently across multiple employers
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume despite being listed as a must-have technical skill; TypeScript/Node.js dominate instead

Evidence of driving technical strategy at organizational scale is weak; staff titles appear but the resume reads as a broad senior IC rather than someone who has owned domain-level technical direction
Stakeholder management and cross-functional coordination — a must-have non-technical skill — are not demonstrated; no mention of working across product, leadership, or incident-response ownership
